arguments.task: Visit official Front pages to verify API availability, authentication methods, developer docs, rate limits, pricing/access, and any official MCP/server references. Prefer front.com official docs first, then official GitHub if linked by Front. Return exact URLs and concise findings only from the pages visited.

input.thought: Start a browser research run on official Front properties to collect verifiable evidence.

API Availability & Developer Docs**\\n*   **Availability:** Core API access is available on **all plans** (Starter, Professional, and Enterprise).\\n*   **Documentation:** Official developer documentation is hosted at [https://dev.frontapp.com/docs/welcome](https://dev.frontapp.com/docs/welcome).\\n*   **Core API Reference:** [https://dev.frontapp.com/reference/introduction](https://dev.frontapp.com/reference/introduction)\\n\\n**2. Authentication Methods**\\n*   **Methods:** Front uses **Bearer Token** authentication. \\n*   **Types:** Supports both **JSON Web Tokens (JWT)** for OAuth-based integrations and **API Tokens** for personal/internal use.\\n*   **MCP Auth:** Specifically uses **OAuth 2.1 with PKCE** for the MCP server.\\n*   **Auth Docs:** [https://dev.frontapp.com/docs/authentication](https://dev.frontapp.com/docs/authentication)\\n\\n**3. raw_result.description: Customer communication platform with shared inboxes, collaboration, and support workflow APIs.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official auth docs state Front uses Bearer token authentication with both OAuth-based integrations and API tokens; MCP docs specifically mention OAuth 2.1 with PKCE.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing indicates developer tools/Core API are included starting on paid Starter plans; advanced 'connect any messaging platform via API' is limited to Professional and Enterprise.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer platform includes Core API, Analytics API, and Channels API; core API appears broadly usable, while custom channel capabilities are narrower and plan-gated.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Front MCP server is documented as open beta at mcp.frontapp.com/mcp and uses OAuth 2.1 with PKCE.

raw_result.blocker: Requires a paid Front plan for self-serve API access; some advanced API capabilities are limited to higher tiers.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs list per-minute limits by plan: Starter 50 RPM, Professional 100 RPM, Enterprise 200 RPM, and OAuth partner integrations 120 RPM. Pricing also advertises a Rate Limit Increase add-on for $200/month.
raw_result.notable_signals: Front has official public developer docs, plan-specific rate limits, a documented MCP server in open beta, and explicit pricing-page signals that core developer tools are included on all paid plans while Channels API-style messaging platform connectivity is more restricted.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Evidence supports paid self-serve API access and REST docs, but the exact classification of OAuth credentials as JWT-based bearer auth and whether all API surfaces beyond core/analytics are equally accessible across plans is somewhat ambiguous from the gathered pages alone.
